ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-26-2015 Continued To Jun-16-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mercy Hall for (Trustee) Brigitte Betcher.

Ms. Hall reported that this is the final accounting of the former trustee, that they have been working to get additional information on the charges, that the charges go back further than the appointment of the current trustee, that they have been unable to get additional information on Eden Prairie but have expressed the need to the client for clarification, that the expenses for the trips to Reno were for the beneficiary and a family member/caregiver only, that the "family gifts" were purchases of toys at Toys R Us which were gifts for the beneficiaries grandson and that these issues will not continue to come up as there has been a professional fiduciary in place since 2/26/13. The Court addressed the time entries for the firm stating that they appeared to be more administrative than legal. Ms. Hall reported that there general staff is unable to do much more than copy, that the fees are for the paralegals preparing attachments, distributing and calendaring from orders received, etc., and clarified that the two time entries in question were in fact separate incidents. The Court reminded counsel that they cannot charge professional fees for non professional work even if it being done by a professional and that any non professional time should not be billed as such. The accounting petition was continued to June 16, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition with regards to fees only was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
